Maple Leafs try to make best of their thin draft
Amber Heard wants Johnny Depp victory tossed over Juror No. 15
'Freedom Convoy' organizer Tamara Lich to remain in jail pending trial   Lich was arrested last week after she appeared in a recent photograph with fellow protest leader Tom Marazzo

Family of Mississauga man sues police, paramedics and SIU over his death   The family of a 28-year-old man killed by police west of Toronto has filed a lawsuit over his death, alleging negligence on the part of the force, local paramedics and the province’s police watchdog.
Ontario expands coverage of cystic fibrosis treatment   Ontario will expand publicly-funded coverage of a ‘transformational’ cystic fibrosis treatment to include kids aged six and older.

World stunned by Abe's assassination in light of Japan's gun laws   TOKYO — The assassination of former Prime Minister Shinzo Abe in broad daylight Friday shocked a world that has come to associate Japan with relatively low crime and strict gun control.

WWE's Vince McMahon body-slammed in $12M 'hush money' pay off   WWE boss Vince McMahon is in a sexual harassment stranglehold that may be tougher to squirm out of than the Iron Shiek’s feared Camel Clutch.

Two for the show: Blue Jays Kirk and Guerrero chosen as all-star game starters   SEATTLE — For Alejandro Kirk, a first trip to the all-star game will be full of ‘oh wow moments,’ as he gets the chance to hang with the best players in the game so early in his career.
TFC acquires Toronto native Kaye in blockbuster trade, with Salcedo likely next to go   Toronto FC is not just cleaning house. The club is taking a blowtorch to its roster.
Unloading Mrazek contract, turning three picks into five, a strong start to off-season for Kyle Dubas   Kyle Dubas got his first summer win out of the way in Montreal.

TRAIKOS: Maple Leafs remain without a goalie — and with fewer options now available   The Colorado Avalanche proved that you can win a Stanley Cup with a 32-year-old former minor-league journeyman in net. But what are the chances you can win a championship with a couple of backups who have combined for less than 20 NHL games?
TRAIKOS: Are the Calgary Flames close to re-signing Johnny Gaudreau?   The clock is ticking.

Redblacks vs Roughriders Week 5 Picks and Predictions: Ottawa Dealt Another Rough Hand   The Ottawa Redblacks face off against the Saskatchewan Roughriders in Week 5 of CFL action on Friday night. The Redblacks are still looking for their first win of the year and it won’t come easy at Mosaic Stadium.

Rafael Nadal vs Nick Kyrgios Picks: It's Kyrgios' Time to Shine   Nick Kyrgios is appearing in his first Grand Slam semifinal and is the favorite against Rafael Nadal, who is struggling to come to terms with an abdomen injury that flared up in his quarterfinal victory.

Novak Djokovic vs Cameron Norrie Picks: First-Time Semifinalist Seeks to Dethrone King   The contrasting tale of champion vs. contender plays out Friday on Centre Court, as Novak Djokovic and Cameron Norrie battle for a place in the Wimbledon final.

Blue Jays vs A's Picks and Predictions: Favourites Make Good   The Toronto Blue Jays and the Oakland A’s will wrap up their three-game series at RingCentral Coliseum with some afternoon baseball this Wednesday.

2022 NHL Draft Odds and Prop Picks: Slafkovsky Goes Right After Wright   The 2022 NHL Draft is here and several teams are looking to find a player who can provide immediate help in the upcoming season. The draft is a very exciting day for players, front offices, and fan bases, but it also comes with a ton of pressure.
True Crime Byline: We should have seen JoAnn Wilson's murder coming   Leader-Post city editor Barb Pacholik recalls the many turns of the Colin Thatcher case across decades.

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.
HAVE YOUR SAY: Answer our daily Toronto Sun poll question

MANNING: Ottawa needs to become a capital where all Canadians feel welcome   According to Wikipedia and numerous tourism pamphlets, “Ottawa is the capital city of Canada”. But for an increasing number of Canadians, Ottawa falls far short of serving or acting like a truly national capital.

GOLDBERG: The huge mistake the Jan. 6 committee could easily avoid   Will the Jan. 6 committee issue a “criminal referral” to the Justice Department for Donald Trump? Committee members can’t give an interview without being asked that question. Pundits can’t stop talking about it, cable TV anchors can’t stop asking legal experts for their predictions. It’s nothing less than a media obsession.
JIVANI: Patrick Brown defends himself by disparaging Conservatives   Oddly, he considers himself uniquely qualified to change a party whose members he holds in such contempt.
OPINION: Dire shortage of nurses must be addressed   Ontario and Canada's health-care system are on the brink of catastrophe

Tony Sirico, who played Paulie Walnuts on 'The Sopranos,' dies at 79   Actor Tony Sirico, known for his role as Paulie Walnuts in the popular American television show ‘The Sopranos,” died Friday at 79, his family and manager announced.
'24' and 'The Mentalist' star Gregory Itzin dead at 74   Gregory Itzin has died at the age of 74.
Joe Rogan claims he snubbed Trump on podcast: 'I DON'T WANT TO HELP HIM'   Podcaster calls former president 'an existential threat to democracy itself'

Doja Cat brands 'Stranger Things' star Noah Schnapp a 'weasel' for sharing DMs   Doja Cat has branded ‘Stranger Things’ star Noah Schnapp a “Weasel” in a foul-mouthed rant after he shared their private messages.

Love and midlife crisis: How Taika Waititi hammered out a new Thor and convinced Chris Hemsworth to get naked   'What's more human than trying to figure out what your purpose is in the universe?' Oscar winner says
Luxury brand Tom Ford hires Goldman Sachs to explore potential sale   A deal could value the company at several billion dollars
ASK AMY: Woman wonders how to nicely repel gym rat   Dear Amy: I am a very active woman.
There are some concert goers who enjoy more than the music when attending events   The lifting of Canada’s pandemic lockdown seems to have unleashed a tidal wave of pent-up social activities.
ASK AMY: Emotional cheating won’t lead to bounce-back   Dear Amy: Six months ago, my fiancee and I split up after 13 years together because of my emotional cheating.

Norwegian Cruise scraps COVID testing for select voyages   Passengers boarding Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings ships won’t have to take COVID-19 tests from next month unless required by local law, the U.S. cruise operator said on Wednesday as the crucial summer sailing season gathers steam.

SUNshine Girl Cassandra   SUNshine Girl Cassandra is back and hopefully living the pampered life. The last time we checked in, she was dreaming of being a rich housewife and driving Bugattis. (Ernest Doroszuk/Toronto Sun)
RAYS OF SUNSHINE: Our Top 5 SUNshine Girls for June 2022   WATCH as we highlight our Top 5 SUNshine Girls for the month of June 2022!

SUNshine Girl Erin   SUNshine Girl Erin loves cosy Italian cars and rap and R&B artists. The 5-foot-10, Sagittarius spends her downtime perusing through Instagram or hitting the club. (Ernest Doroszuk/Toronto Sun)

SUNshine Girl Shelby   SUNshine Girl Shelby, a 5-foot-3, brown-eyed Taurus, loves to play with her dogs. She's a big fan of Taylor Swift and also loves to sing, dance and play volleyball. (Toronto Sun/Postmedia Network)
SUNshine Girl Ally   SUNshine Girl Ally is a 5-foot-4 blue-eyed Libra. She loves to golf and listen to the Deftones, Motley Crue and Def Leppard. (Toronto Sun/Postmedia Network)
GMC announces 2023 Sierra 1500 AT4X AEV Edition   Factory-installed heavy-duty parts will make it the most capable off-road Sierra yet
Hold Your Horses: E.U. mandates speed limiters on certain light vehicles   Proponents say installing the tech could be the first step in eliminating speeding on European roads
Ford issues fire-risk recall for Escape, Maverick, and Lincoln Corsair   The automaker is also expanding a recall on 2021 Ford Expedition and Lincoln Navigator SUVs, advising they be parked outside
Dodge may debut Demon-topping E85-capable Hellcat: report   Could this farewell to the monster motor see even more power than the psychotic 840-hp Challenger Demon drag special?
Hyundai teases performance vehicles ahead of 'N Day'   And we know that at least one of them, and maybe two, will be all-electric

'ALS will be a treatable chronic disease': New treatment slows disease progression and increases life expectancy   McGill University's Dr. Angela Genge is optimistic about what the future holds for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis care: "Every step gives our patients more time, better function for longer, better care every year."
Asking For A Friend: Are essential oils worth the hype?   The oils from plants offer natural healing and cleansing benefits, but they can also be dangerous.
Our friends may be our friends because of the way they smell   When it comes to judging other people on their body odour, we may be more like other mammals than we realize, according to a new study.
TikTok Tuesday: #healthycoke — a trend that’s not so healthy for your teeth (WATCH)   It may be low in sugar, but carbonated water mixed with vinegar is a terrible choice for your teeth.
Putting our phones on silent mode only makes them more tempting, study says   Blame FOMO for how hard it can be for us to stop looking at our phones all the time.
Ontario police seize cannabis worth $61 million   Estimated 45,000-plus illegal pot plants and eight metric tonnes of processed weed confiscated.

Joe Rogan responds to border drug seizure: ‘You should take all these drugs and rethink your life’   Border agents seized small amounts of ecstasy, cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms from a vehicle entering New York.
One of Canada's biggest universities will have a pot store on campus   The planned store would represent the pot company Burb's eighth and final one allowed in the province under its retail cap.
Ontario casinos laundered $3 million in illegal cannabis proceeds: RCMP   Weed worth approx. $24.8 million confiscated.

Man's ploy to flush over 20 kilograms of cannabis down the toilet leads him straight to jail   Officers also found 21 kilograms of weed, additional bags of prepackaged marijuana, a handgun and over $16K in cash.
